code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test13-OCT-2019Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function, and metabolic acidosis (low CO₂). Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, bilirubinuria, and low specific gravity, indicating possible tubular dysfunction and/or glomerular damage. No evidence of urinary infection. Low HbA1c with glucosuria may suggest renal glycosuria or recent glycemic changes. Findings are consistent with significant renal impairment; further nephrological evaluation is recommended.
